title: Best Original Screenplay Nominees at the 2026 Oscars, Ranked
text: According to Collider's rankings, here are the five nominees for Best Original Screenplay at the 2026 Oscars, from lowest to highest: 5. 'Blue Moon' (Robert Kaplow) - Kaplow's debut screenplay about Lorenz Hart attempts to balance a "character that is overly verbose, pretentious, and ultimately tragic" while keeping audiences engaged. The reviewer notes it's "impressive for a first screenplay" but ranks as the weakest of the five nominees. 4. 'It Was Just an Accident' (Jafar Panahi) - This Iranian film marks Panahi's return to narrative features. The screenplay "feels like it could've only been written by Panahi," exploring themes of revenge and societal recovery with dark humor grounded in real consequences. 3. 'Sinners' (Ryan Coogler) - Coogler's screenplay demonstrates brilliance in how it "constantly shifts and changes" from a juke joint story into a vampire narrative. The reviewer acknowledges the script's strength while noting the final film's visual elements enhance the written work. 2. 'Marty Supreme' (Ronald Bronstein & Josh Safdie) - This 168-page screenplay about an ambitious table tennis player "flies by" with propulsive momentum. The writers excel at maintaining reader engagement throughout the narrative's twists. 1. 'Sentimental Value' (Eskil Vogt & Joachim Trier) - The top-ranked screenplay interweaves a filmmaker's story with his daughters' narratives and a house's century-long history. It represents "the most complex script" that successfully juggles multiple narrative layers without feeling cluttered.
